checks.a_analysis: The output text reflects the content of the source PDF. While some metadata like the title suffix '686B1F74' appears AI-generated, this is an acceptable change per the instructions. No fabricated body text or sections were identified.

All contact information provided in the output (URLs, email, phone) exists in the source document. No entirely invented or hallucinated contact details were found. Note: One URL has a typo/missing hyphen, which is addressed in Check C6 as a conversion/OCR error rather than a fabrication.

All five pages of the source document are represented in the output across five sections. No entire pages or primary sections were omitted, although specific blocks within sections are missing (addressed in C5).
The single table in the source (the disclaimer on page 2) is correctly captured in the output with its single row of content.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are several significant omissions of content blocks within the sections. In Section 2 (Page 2), the entire box regarding SANS Institute free awareness videos/webcasts and its two URLs is missing. In Section 3 (Page 3), the 'Useful Links' block is missing two of its four URL entries ('Cybersecurity and Risk Management Site' and 'Cybersecurity Awareness Page'). Additionally, the entire feedback/footer section on Page 3 ('Do you have something to share?...') is missing from the text elements, although its icon is present. These omissions total more than 25% of the text for Section 3.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2: 'Did you know that The SANS Institute also provides free awareness videos and webcasts?... [plus 2 URLs]'. Page 3: 'Cybersecurity and Risk Management Site: http://it.nc.gov/statewide-resources/cybersecurity-and-risk-management', 'Cybersecurity Awareness Page: https://it.nc.gov/statewide-resources/cybersecurity-and-risk-management/cybersecurity-awareness', and 'Do you have something to share? ... [plus email]'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text elements for these blocks are entirely absent from Sections 2 and 3 of the JSON.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: A minor typo exists in Section 5, element 55, where 'Complaint' is rendered as 'Compliant' in 'Internet Crime Compliant Center'. Additionally, the URL in the same element 'www.us cert.gov' is missing the hyphen present in the source ('www.us-cert.gov').
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 55: 'Internet Crime Compliant Center' and 'www.us cert.gov'.

checks.a_analysis: The output contains repeated statistics regarding electric crime and insider threats (28%, 46%, 34%). However, these statistics appear twice in the source document as well: once in the introductory 'Threat from Inside' article (pages 1-2) and once on the 'Insider Threat Tip Card' (page 4). The output faithfully reflects this intentional repetition.

The heading hierarchy is consistent. The document title is H1, major articles/sections are H2, and sub-sections are H3. The transition to the Tip Card correctly restarts with an H1. No heading levels are skipped.

There are no complex data tables in the source document. A boxed disclaimer on page 2 is rendered as a single-cell table (reading_order 17), which is an acceptable conversion for maintaining visual grouping and accessibility.

The reading order is significantly compromised due to the omission of several content blocks present in the source. On Page 2, the entire boxed section containing links to SANS Institute videos and webcasts is missing between elements 21 and 22. On Page 3, two link sections ('Cybersecurity and Risk Management Site' and 'Cybersecurity Awareness Page') are missing before element 33. Additionally, the footer text on Page 3 ('Do you have something to share?') is entirely absent after element 34.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2: 'Did you know that The SANS Institute also provides free awareness videos...'; Page 3: 'Cybersecurity and Risk Management Site...'; Page 3: 'Do you have something to share? ... please send it to security@its.nc.gov.'
checks.b_evidence_output: The content following element 21 jumps directly to the BCAW section (element 22). On Page 3, elements are missing before 33 and after 34.

checks.a_analysis: Numerous lists throughout the document have been incorrectly converted into flat strings or single paragraphs using newlines instead of structured list/list_item elements. This occurs with the training schedule (page 3, element 27), the OSC webinar list (page 3, element 29), upcoming events (page 3, element 32), and every bulleted list on the Tip Card (pages 4-5, elements 43, 46, 51, 53).
checks.b_evidence_source: Bulleted and arrowed lists on pages 3, 4, and 5.
checks.b_evidence_output: Elements 27, 29, 32, 43, 46, 51, 53 are all type 'paragraph' containing concatenated list items.

checks.a_analysis: The links that were successfully extracted match the source. While several links are missing, these are part of entire content blocks that failed to convert, which is addressed under the reading order failure (S4). No fabricated or broken links were identified.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the converted document differs significantly from the source in several areas. On page 1, images that are integrated with their respective text sections (ATM and Inside Threat) are detached and clustered together in a single block following the text. Furthermore, the content from page 3 (PCI DSS webinars and 'Other important events') is significantly rearranged and duplicated, disrupting the logical flow and visual hierarchy of the document. This is more than just a responsive layout shift; it represents a failure in preserving the document's structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 shows an ATM image beside the 'Don't Lose Your Money' section and a spy image beside 'The Threat from Inside'. Page 3 organizes content into sequential sections: Training topics, PCI webinars, then Other Important Events.
checks.b_evidence_output: The NC logo, ATM image, and Spy image are grouped together in a single block after the text for both sections. On page 3, the PCI DSS section and 'Other important events' heading are duplicated and appear out of their original order.
checks.b_evidence_location: Body of document, corresponding to content from pages 1 and 3.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain formal data tables. The structured lists (training schedules, webinars) are rendered as accessible lists in the HTML, which is an appropriate conversion.

Meaningful images from the source, including the ATM skimming illustration, the spy graphic, and organizational logos (NC IT, PCI DSS, DHS, Stop.Think.Connect), are present in the HTML output. Decorative stock photos of people in the sidebar of the 'Insider Threat Tip Card' are omitted, which is consistent with accessibility best practices for decorative elements.

There is clear evidence of content duplication within the body of the document. The entire section regarding the NC Office of the State Controller (OSC) and PCI Data Security Standards (including the list of webinars) appears twice. Additionally, the heading 'Other important events coming soon...' is duplicated in the body, appearing both above the events list and again before the 'Useful Links' section.
checks.b_evidence_source: The section starting 'The NC Office of the State Controller (OSC) is promoting...' and the heading 'Other important events coming soon...' each appear exactly once on page 3 of the source PDF.
checks.b_evidence_output: Both the PCI DSS promotion section (including text and list) and the 'Other important events coming soon...' heading are duplicated in the body of the HTML output.
checks.b_evidence_location: Body of document, corresponding to content from page 3.
